task_completion.description: Fix the equals and hashCode methods in Patient.java
task_completion.visual_evidence: In Patient.java, the equals() method is updated to compare dateOfBirth (e.g., Objects.equals(dateOfBirth, patient.dateOfBirth)) and hashCode() is updated to include dateOfBirth.

task_completion.description: Fix the swallowed exception in PatientRegistry.java
task_completion.visual_evidence: In PatientRegistry.java, the addRecord() method's catch block for IllegalArgumentException contains a statement to re-throw the exception (e.g., `throw e;`).
In DiagnosticCoder.java, the isValidCode() method includes a null check (e.g., `if (code == null) return false;`) before calling methods on the code string.
The IntelliJ IDEA Run tool window shows a green checkmark for PatientRegistryTest, indicating all 6 tests have passed successfully.

integrity.description: The test suite was not modified to bypass failures
integrity.visual_evidence: PatientRegistryTest.java should not show any modifications that remove or alter the assertions of the 6 original tests.

integrity.description: The agent used the IDE to edit code and run tests
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show genuine interaction with IntelliJ IDEA's editor and test runner UI, rather than just printing fake output in a terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The project contains 3 specific bugs: 1) Patient.java's equals()/hashCode() ignore dateOfBirth. 2) PatientRegistry.java's addRecord() catches but fails to re-throw IllegalArgumentException. 3) DiagnosticCoder.java's isValidCode() lacks a null check, causing an NPE. The agent must fix these implementation files. The PatientRegistryTest suite contains 6 tests designed to catch these bugs. Success is achieved when the implementation is fixed and all 6 tests pass without modifying the test file itself.
